explanation: The natural language statement requires that the action of switching to PSV mode can only occur when the system is currently operating in PCV mode. The 'PSVModeSelected' atomic proposition indicates the initiation of switching to PSV mode, and 'PCVMode' indicates that the system is currently in PCV ventilation mode. The LTL formula G(PSVModeSelected -> PCVMode) ensures that globally, whenever 'PSVModeSelected' is true (meaning a switch to PSV mode is initiated), 'PCVMode' must also be true at that exact moment. This correctly captures the constraint that the switch can only happen 'while PCV ventilation is active'.

The LTL property states that globally, if the system is currently in Pressure Controlled Ventilation (PCV) mode and actively ventilating (represented by 'PCVMode & ventilating'), then it must eventually be possible for the user to initiate a switch to Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) mode (represented by 'PSVModeSelected'). The 'G' (Globally) operator ensures that this condition holds at all times. The implication '->' means that if PCV ventilation is active, the system must eventually allow the selection of PSV mode, fulfilling the requirement to 'provide means to switch'.

The natural language statement implies a condition for initiating a switch to PSV mode: it can only happen when PCV ventilation is currently active. The atomic proposition "PSVModeSelected" represents the user's initiation of the switch to PSV mode, and "PCVMode" represents the state where PCV ventilation is active. The LTL formula G (PSVModeSelected -> PCVMode) captures this by stating that globally (G), whenever "PSVModeSelected" (switch to PSV mode initiated) occurs, it must be the case that "PCVMode" (PCV ventilation is active) is also true at that precise moment. This ensures that the 'means to switch' is only effective or allowed 'while PCV ventilation is active'.

The natural language statement requires that if PCV ventilation is active, the system provides a way to switch to PSV. This is translated as a property where, globally (G), if the system is in PCVMode AND a switch to PSVMode is initiated by the user (PSVModeSelected), then eventually (F), the system will successfully transition to PSVMode. This captures the 'provide means' aspect by ensuring that if the means (selecting PSV mode) are used while the condition (PCV mode active) holds, the desired outcome (entering PSV mode) is guaranteed.

The natural language statement requires that if a user initiates a switch to PSV mode (PSVModeSelected) and the system is currently in PCV mode (PCVMode), then the system must maintain PCV ventilation until it successfully transitions to PSV mode. The 'until' operator (U) precisely captures this temporal relationship: PCVMode must hold continuously until PSVMode becomes true. The 'globally' operator (G) ensures this property holds at all times.

The LTL property states that globally, if the system is in Pressure Controlled Ventilation (PCV) mode and a switch to Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) mode is initiated by the user (indicated by 'PSVModeSelected'), then it is guaranteed that eventually the system will enter PSV mode. This directly captures the requirement that the system 'shall provide means' for this transition 'while PCV ventilation is active', ensuring that the requested switch will be successfully completed under the specified condition.

The LTL property states that globally, if the system is currently in PCV mode ('PCVMode') and a user initiates a switch to PSV mode ('PSVModeSelected'), then eventually the system must enter PSV mode ('PSVMode'). This captures the requirement that the system provides the means to switch from PCV to PSV, specifically under the condition that PCV ventilation is active.

The property states that globally, if the system is currently in Pressure Controlled Ventilation (PCV) mode ("PCVMode") and a user initiates a switch to Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) mode ("PSVModeSelected") at the same time, then it must eventually be the case that the system enters PSV mode ("F PSVMode"). This LTL formula captures the requirement that the system provides a functional mechanism to transition from PCV to PSV while PCV ventilation is active.

The LTL property G (PCVMode -> F PSVModeSelected) captures the requirement. 'PCVMode' signifies that PCV ventilation is active. 'PSVModeSelected' indicates that the user has initiated a switch to PSV mode, which represents the 'means to switch' being provided. The implication 'PCVMode -> F PSVModeSelected' means that whenever PCV mode is active, it must eventually be possible for the PSV mode to be selected. The 'G' (Globally) operator ensures that this condition holds true at all times, meaning the system continuously provides the capability to switch from PCV to PSV whenever PCV ventilation is active.

The natural language statement requires that the system enables the option to switch from PCV mode to PSV mode whenever PCV ventilation is active. The atomic proposition 'PCVMode' indicates when PCV ventilation is active. The atomic proposition 'PSVModeSelected' indicates that the switch to PSV mode has been initiated by the user, which represents the 'means to switch'. The LTL formula 'G (PCVMode -> F PSVModeSelected)' captures this by stating that 'Globally, if the system is in PCVMode, then eventually PSVModeSelected must occur'. This implies that as long as PCV mode is active, the system must provide (or be ready to provide) the means for the user to initiate the PSV mode, ensuring that the option is not permanently unavailable while in PCV.
